differentials.name: muscle composed of (fast contracting) type II fibers
differentials.raw: muscle composed of (fast contracting) type II fibers

differentials.name: no loss of muscle function Path:   interstitial edema + hemorrhage at myotendinous junction with extension into adjacent muscle fibers
differentials.raw: no loss of muscle function Path:   interstitial edema + hemorrhage at myotendinous junction with extension into adjacent muscle fibers

context: Cause:  direct trauma, usually by blunt object Site:     deep within muscle belly √  NO architectural changes √  feathery appearance of diffuse muscle edema √  increased muscle girth √  deep intramuscular hematoma (with severe trauma resulting in disruption of muscle fibers): √  high SI on T1WI (= T1 shortening of methemoglobin) √  low SI on T2WI (= T2 shortening of hemosiderin) √  blooming with gradient-echo sequence Myotendinous Strain Cause:  single traumatic event from excessive stretching Susceptibility factors: Site:     myotendinous junction (= weakest point of musculotendinous unit) Cl
